archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Root-level CI pipeline orchestration entry point
Integration layer for Claude API/CLI interactions
Adaptive analysis business logic for agent behavior
Agent completion handling and result processing
Prompt construction service for agent planning
Type contracts and interface definitions for the guard layer
Path resolution and validation utilities for guards
Explicitly named gateway — re-exports cross-package imports from rex
Metrics collection utility for concurrent executions
Concurrency/rate limiting infrastructure shared across the process layer
Memory monitoring infrastructure used by other process modules
Memory-based throttling helper for the process layer
RuntimePool class — reusable worker pool infrastructure with acquire/release lifecycle
Tracks per-process memory, shared infrastructure utility
ExecutionQueue class — bounded FIFO queue with priority scheduling, shared infrastructure
Formatting helpers for queue display output
Interface-only file: ToolGuard, ToolContext, RexToolHandlers — no logic
Orchestrates tool dispatch — routes named tool calls to implementations, defines TOOL_DEFINITIONS
Low-level shell execution helper used by tool implementations
Rex tool handlers with business logic: status updates, log appending, subtask creation, auto-completion
Test execution helper used by the agent tool layer
Completion validation logic — checks git diff and runs tests before allowing task completion
Anthropic SDK API client with retry logic and error classification
Auth mode detection and API key resolution helpers
Client factory utility that wires together providers based on config
Vendor-neutral LLM client factory — top-level service entry point selecting claude vs codex
LLM vendor type definitions and enums — no logic
Project-level config loading for the llm-client package
Pure constants and type definitions — PROJECT_DIRS object and ProjectDir type with no logic.
Interface and type definitions only: LLMProvider, ProviderInfo, StreamChunk, ProviderCapability, ProviderAuthMode.
ProviderRegistry class manages factory registration and config-driven provider instantiation — business logic orchestration.
ProviderSession class manages active provider state and vendor switching — stateful service lifecycle management.
Shared CLI utility functions: Levenshtein edit distance, suggestCommands, formatTypoSuggestion — pure helpers with high fan-in potential.
Stateless parsing helpers for token usage across API, CLI, and stream-json formats — no side effects, high reuse.
Manages acknowledged findings store: I/O, hash computation, and CRUD operations — data persistence with business logic.
Shared constants, token usage helpers, JSON extraction utilities, and format detection — foundational module imported by many siblings.
Post-processing pipeline that detects over-granular proposals and triggers secondary LLM consolidation calls.
LoE-threshold-driven decomposition pipeline: builds prompts, calls LLM, parses and recursively decomposes oversized tasks.
Deduplication algorithm using bigram Dice, word overlap, and action-verb normalization — pure computation with no I/O.
Pure diff formatting utility comparing proposals against an existing PRD tree — no I/O, returns formatted string.
Multi-phase requirements extraction pipeline: parses documents, classifies headings, builds proposals, optionally uses LLM.
File format validation helpers: extension checks, magic byte detection, content type validation — shared input guards.
Interactive guided spec builder with clarification loop, LLM calls, and readline prompts — orchestrates a multi-turn session.
LLM client bridge: manages module-level config/client singleton, exposes spawnClaude — API client with state.
LLM-powered proposal modification pipeline: builds prompts, calls LLM with retries, validates output.
Proposal builder: groups scan results into epic/feature/task hierarchy with priority sorting and deduplication.
Core analysis reasoning engine with LLM-driven proposal generation; also re-exports from analyze-shared and llm-bridge for backward compatibility.
Reconciles scan results against existing PRD using fuzzy matching to identify already-tracked vs new items.
LLM-powered PRD reshape reasoning: builds prompts from PRD state, parses structured JSON proposals via Zod.
File scanners that traverse the project tree and produce structured ScanResult objects — data-fetching service layer.
Pure validation functions for natural-language modification requests — no I/O, all functions testable in isolation.
CLI help content definitions and formatter — static content module shared across commands.
MCP tool handler implementations: each function implements one tool's business logic against the PRD store.
Sets up and runs the MCP server with stdio transport — CLI entry point for the mcp subcommand.
Shared CLI input validation helpers with consistent error formatting — pure guards imported by command handlers.
Pure conflict detection functions using similarity scoring — no I/O, stateless, shared across recommendation paths.
Atomic PRD item creation from recommendations: validates, creates, and persists items with all-or-nothing semantics.
Single exported string constant defining the default agent workflow — companion content file with no logic.
Defines the archetype vocabulary catalog with weighted signal patterns — pure type/constant data, no logic
Business logic orchestration — classifies completed PRD items by change significance using heuristic rules
Data fetching and orchestration — identifies completed rex PRD items by diffing against base branch
Helper module — filters branch-scoped work items by timestamp and lifecycle metadata
Persistent JSON storage for branch work records with Zod validation — high fan-in state layer
Business logic — generates deterministic architectural findings (god functions, tight coupling, dead code) from call graph
Analysis engine — deterministic call graph builder using TypeScript compiler API for AST parsing
Classification engine — matches weighted signals against archetype catalog to assign file roles
API client — LLM bridge wrapping @n-dx/llm-client for vendor-aware Claude/Codex access
Data fetching — reads prd.json to extract completion status and per-item metadata for branch tracking
Analysis phase — scans codebase to build React component catalog
Output generation — produces CONTEXT.md from analyzed zone and inventory data
Orchestration — manages batched AI enrichment passes over zones
Configuration constants — batch sizes, concurrency limits, and per-zone thresholds for enrichment
Parsing helper — extracts findings from AI enrichment responses with markdown fence handling
Business logic — drives per-zone AI enrichment flow and result merging
Top-level enrichment orchestrator — coordinates all AI enrichment passes across zones
Analysis phase — builds import dependency graph from source files
Analysis phase — produces file inventory with metadata
Output generation — assembles llms.txt from analysis results for AI consumption
Shared algorithm — Louvain community detection for zone graph partitioning, reusable across callers
Manages manifest reads/writes and version metadata — orchestrates persistent manifest state
Business logic — derives prioritized agent-ready next steps from zone findings with grouping and sorting
Business logic — computes architectural risk scores and governance findings for zones
Detection engine — identifies HTTP routes from JSDoc, framework calls, and manual routing patterns
Shared helper — token usage tracking and formatting utilities used across analysis phases
Aggregation engine — merges pre-analyzed repos into unified sourcevision output
Business logic — resolves external npm imports to sibling workspace members as ZoneCrossing entries
Workspace analyzer — detects and promotes pre-analyzed sub-directories
Single-purpose helper — hashes zone content for cache invalidation, extracted to break circular dependency
Pure data transformation functions for generating per-zone output files (context.md, summary.json) with no side-effecting service state.
Core zone detection engine orchestrating Louvain community detection, cohesion/coupling metric computation, and zone quality validation.
Stores and formats help text definitions for all CLI commands; no logic beyond formatting.
MCP server factory and tool/resource registration — creates and configures the MCP server with all exposed tools; uncalled exports are expected.
CLI command handler for `sourcevision serve` that spawns the web package viewer.
PDF generation utility transforming sourcevision analysis data into a structured report using pdfkit.
Pure transformation functions that render PR review markdown from branch work records — no state, no side effects.
Chunk merging helpers that deduplicate and recompute summaries across chunked analysis outputs; includes circular dependency detection.
Minimal path normalization helper for cross-platform slash consistency.
Canonical deterministic sorting functions for all output array types to ensure reproducible, git-friendly JSON.
Stateful caching layer with filesystem fingerprinting, LRU eviction, and auto-invalidation for token usage aggregation results.
Stateful time-series metrics collector tracking concurrent process counts, RSS memory, and per-task resource usage in ring buffers.
Re-export-only module concentrating all sourcevision cross-package imports for the web package.
Stateful incremental aggregator for per-task token usage with mtime+size change detection and delta updates.
Port availability detection and dynamic allocation utilities — pure infrastructure helpers with no persistent state.
Diagnostic error classifier and remediation guide for PR markdown failures; pure pattern-matching logic with no state.
Centralized synchronous read/write helpers for prd.json with schema validation — a thin I/O utility, not a stateful service.
Stateful per-process RSS memory tracker with sample history, leak detection, and high-RSS warnings.
Single-purpose scheduler registration wrapper that wires up lifecycle dependencies — no persistent state of its own.
Re-export-only module concentrating all rex cross-package imports (types, constants, utilities, MCP factory) for the web package.
HTTP handlers for analyze, proposals, smart-add, and batch-import endpoints.
HTTP handlers managing epic-by-epic execution state machine with pause/resume and WebSocket progress broadcasting.
HTTP handlers for PRD structure health scoring and reorganization proposal routes.
HTTP handlers for full item CRUD lifecycle — add, get, patch, delete, bulk update, and merge.
HTTP handlers for prune preview and execution with archive management.
HTTP handlers for read-only PRD views — full document, stats, dashboard, next task, and execution log.
HTTP handlers for requirements CRUD and traceability analysis.
Shared helper functions adapting rex API to route patterns — thin wrappers with auto-timestamp transitions.
Stateful in-memory inverted index for full-text PRD search with lazy build, mtime staleness detection, and relevance scoring.
Zone-neutral type definitions (TaskUsageAccumulator, CollectAllIdsFn, audit types) with no logic — purely types and constants.
Service module handling task usage tracking logic
Background scheduler service for cleaning up usage data
WebSocket server setup and connection management
Shared constants/definitions for data file paths
Shared utility for culling/filtering graph nodes
Shared utility for deduplicating concurrent requests
Data loading service for the viewer
HTTP fetch pipeline orchestration for viewer messaging
Utility for coalescing multiple messages into batches
WebSocket messaging pipeline for viewer communication
Utility for detecting and handling crashes
Utility gating DOM updates based on performance
Utility managing graceful degradation under load
Utility buffering and gating responses for performance
Utility batching DOM/state updates for performance
Service orchestrating polling lifecycle and intervals
State container for polling status and configuration
Utility tracking browser tab visibility for polling
Utility managing tick timer for polling intervals
Standalone infrastructure module that manages tab visibility-based timer suspension/resumption — pure logic with no framework dependencies, high reuse potential across the polling pipeline.
Handles schema version migration logic — applies transformations to data to ensure compatibility between schema versions; orchestration/business logic rather than pure types or utilities.
Shared path display helpers with backward-compatible re-exports from the visualization layer; classic utility/helper module with high fan-in.
Zone color palette and lookup helpers — pure data transformation functions with no side effects, used across multiple visualization components.
Flow-diagram data builders that transform zone/call/import data into edge and node arrays for visualization components — pure transformation logic.
Single metric classification helper function — a focused pure utility for meter display logic.
PR validation pipeline that orchestrates build and PRD validation steps, running subprocesses and aggregating results — service-layer orchestration logic.
Refreshes dashboard artifact metadata by inspecting sourcevision output files and writing a structured artifact JSON — data orchestration service.
Translates CLI flags into a validated execution plan (step list, skipped steps, notes) — pure business logic orchestration without I/O.
Snapshot, validate, and rollback utilities for refresh operations — stateful service logic managing file-level consistency across refresh pipeline steps.

Server orchestration module managing start/stop/status lifecycle, PID files, port resolution, and background/foreground modes — exported runWeb function makes it a reusable service rather than a CLI entrypoint.
